title: abies balsamea subsp. fraseri
intro: Abies balsamea subsp. fraseri, also known as Fraser fir, is a coniferous tree species native to the Appalachian Mountains in North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Fraser fir include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. The plant's essential oil is often used in herbal preparations, such as aromatherapy, inhalers, and topical creams, to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
